summary: Completed Gate 1 production slice by adding structured RVV low-precision measurement diagnosis and policy handoff facts for the accepted packed-i4 Gate 4 outcome. Policy evaluation now builds decisions from the handoff, preserving correctness support for accepted regression/no-win evidence while rejecting stale measurement, stale sibling route measurement, and performance-preferred/win-promotion attempts before dispatch preference can be authorized.
continuation: Gate 2: make Gearbox/resource-aware selected-body realization or primitive-chain planning consume the structured measurement diagnosis as remediation input, or fail closed with a precise missing resource/schedule blocker.

summary: Completed Gate 2 production slice by making RVV low-precision resource planning consume the structured measurement policy handoff as a provider-owned remediation decision. The packed-i4 resource selection now carries remediation contract, diagnosis, accepted measurement identity, resource action, dispatch consequence, and no-win/regression blocker facts derived from RVVLowPrecisionPerformancePolicyHandoff. Selected-body realization attrs, pass-fact derivation, route descriptions, and target artifact mirror validation must preserve those facts or fail closed.
continuation: Gate 3: lift the Gate 2 remediation decision into the full provider route fact and target artifact validation surface without metadata authority; Gates 4-5 remain for new same-target measurement evidence and dispatch/performance policy enablement.

summary: Completed Gate 3 provider-target validation mirror slice by carrying low-precision resource route-family plan and provider-supported mirror facts on the provider-owned resource selection, deriving them from the validated RVV contraction route-family plan, requiring route descriptions and selected descriptions to match those provider facts, exporting them as target artifact mirrors, and fail-closing stale or missing candidate metadata before artifact acceptance.
continuation: Gate 4: generate and measure the remediated low-precision artifact on the same ssh rvv target with structured evidence tied to provider-owned resource, route-family, primitive-chain, and remediation facts; Gate 5 remains for dispatch/performance policy consumption after accepted measurement evidence.

summary: Completed Gate 4 same-target generated-artifact measurement acceptance slice by binding accepted or rejected measurement evidence to provider-owned resource, route-family, primitive-chain, remediation, target artifact, ABI/runtime, and same-target facts. The target support bundle now exports remediation mirror facts into generated headers, the ABI E2E path expects route-family/provider/remediation metadata in artifact indexes and headers, and the same-target measurement script reads verified target artifact metadata before route summaries while rejecting stale or missing Gate 4 provider/resource/remediation facts.
runtime_evidence: Generated and measured artifacts/tmp/codex-gate4-remediation-real/gate4-remediation-packed-i4-ssh on ssh rvv: 12 summaries, 60 measurements, classification=regression, best_speedup_range=0.691667..0.705064, selection_eligible=false, claim_allowed=false.
continuation: Gate 5: make dispatch/performance policy consume the accepted Gate 4 no-win/regression classification while preserving correctness support and denying performance preference/win claims.
